customer_anxiety_profile: Bobby is a working plumber — practical, cost-focused, time-constrained. His primary gate is cost transparency before commitment. He is not hostile to solar; he has done some prior research (credits awareness, tax credit question) but will not move forward without a clearer cost picture. He deferred, not rejected.
unresolved_concerns: Total system cost — Bobby asked twice and received only deflections
Whether the tax credit meaningfully reduces his out-of-pocket cost
No callback time locked — follow-up is entirely unstructured
sensitive_topics_raised: Bobby was mid-job during the call (emergency plumbing call in Inwood) — he was not passively avoiding, he was genuinely unavailable
Carter made a 'paying nothing out of pocket to get started' claim that may not hold given current tax credit disclosure language
exact_promises_rep_made: Carter promised to send his email address via text
Carter gave Bobby his personal phone number via text
Carter implied follow-up would come 'in a week' via email
closer_should_know: Bobby showed two unsolicited enthusiasm signals (solar credits at 01:00, tax credit question at 03:38) and volunteered his home size (2,000 sq ft) — he is warmer than the call's outcome suggests. The re-contact hook is the tax credit question he asked on the way out. Carter's 'paying nothing out of pocket' claim needs to be addressed carefully; do not repeat it without verifying current tax credit eligibility. Bobby has made no time commitment — re-contact should open with a specific callback time offer, not a general check-in.
